description.en: Master Python's logging module — levels, formatters, handlers, structured logging with JSON, and best practices for production observability.
description.ta: Python-ன் logging module கற்றுக்கொள்ளுங்கள் — levels, formatters, handlers, JSON-உடன் structured logging, production observability-க்கான best practices.

story_variants.en:
## Vaathiyaar's Black Box Recorder

Vaathiyaar holds up a flight recorder. "Every airplane has a black box. If something goes wrong, investigators open it and read the logs. Your Python app needs the same thing. When your server crashes at 3 AM, logs tell you WHY."

### Why Not Just print()?
- `print()` goes to stdout only — lost when the process ends
- No severity levels — you can't filter what matters
- No timestamps, no source info
- Can't route to files, services, or monitoring tools

### Logging Levels

| Level | Value | Use For |
|-------|-------|---------|
| DEBUG | 10 | Detailed debugging info |
| INFO | 20 | General operational events |
| WARNING | 30 | Something unexpected but not fatal |
| ERROR | 40 | Something failed |
| CRITICAL | 50 | System is going down |

### Structured Logging
- JSON format for machine-readable logs
- Key-value pairs for easy filtering
- Essential for log aggregation (ELK, Datadog, Cloud Logging)

### Best Practices
- Use `__name__` as logger name
- Never log sensitive data (passwords, tokens)
- Use appropriate levels — not everything is ERROR
- Include context: request_id, user_id, operation

> **Key Concept:** Logging is your application's memory. Good logs tell a story — what happened, when, where, and why. In production, logs are your eyes and ears.

explanation.en: Import the built-in logging module — no pip install needed
Create a logger for this module
Use __name__ so the logger is named after the module (e.g., 'app.utils')
Set minimum level to DEBUG — process all messages
Create a formatter to control log output format
Define how each log line looks
Include timestamp, logger name, level, and the message
Create handlers to send logs to different destinations
Send logs to a file — persists after the process ends
File only gets WARNING and above — reduces file size
Apply our format to the file handler
Now use the logger in your code
Attach the file handler to the logger
Log an INFO message — general operational event

left.label: print() (BAD)
left.content:
print('Error: DB connection failed')
# No timestamp
# No level
# Lost when process ends
# Can't filter or route

right.label: logging (GOOD)
right.content:
logger.error('DB connection failed',
  extra={'host': 'db01', 'retry': 3})
# 2026-03-27 10:15:32 - app - ERROR
# - DB connection failed

output: 2026-03-27 10:15:30 - app - DEBUG - Loading config from config.yaml
2026-03-27 10:15:32 - app.auth - WARNING - Login attempt with expired token
2026-03-27 10:15:33 - app.api - ERROR - Request failed: timeout after 30s
2026-03-27 10:15:33 - app.api - ERROR - Traceback (most recent call last):
2026-03-27 10:15:32 - app.auth - WARNING - Login attempt with expired token
2026-03-27 10:15:33 - app.api - ERROR - Request failed: timeout after 30s

instruction.en: Set up a logger with: 1) A StreamHandler for console output at DEBUG level, 2) A FileHandler for 'errors.log' at ERROR level, 3) A formatter with timestamp and level. Then log messages at each level.
instruction.ta: ஒரு logger அமையுங்கள்: 1) DEBUG level-ல் console output-க்கு StreamHandler, 2) ERROR level-ல் 'errors.log'-க்கு FileHandler, 3) timestamp மற்றும் level-உடன் formatter. பிறகு ஒவ்வொரு level-லும் messages log செய்யுங்கள்.

practice_challenges.starter_code:
import logging

# Create logger
logger = logging.getLogger('myapp')
logger.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)

# Create formatter
formatter = logging.Formatter(
    '%(asctime)s - %(name)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s'
)

# Create console handler (DEBUG level)
console_handler = logging.StreamHandler()
# Set level and formatter

# Create file handler (ERROR level)
# Your code here

# Add handlers to logger
# Your code here

# Log at each level
logger.debug('This is a debug message')
logger.info('Application started')
logger.warning('Disk space low')
logger.error('Database connection failed')
logger.critical('System shutting down')
practice_challenges.expected_output: Console shows all 5 messages, errors.log shows only ERROR and CRITICAL

q.en: Why use logging instead of print()?
q.ta: print()-க்கு பதிலாக logging ஏன் பயன்படுத்த வேண்டும்?

en: Logging provides levels, timestamps, and routing to files/services
ta: Logging levels, timestamps, files/services-க்கு routing வழங்குகிறது

q.en: What logging level should you use for 'user login successful'?
q.ta: 'user login successful'-க்கு எந்த logging level பயன்படுத்த வேண்டும்?

en: Names the logger after the current module
Creates a file with that name
